events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes pipe bedding & backfill work where lifts are too thick for the compactor. This is a compaction issue - backfill lifts must be placed in thin enough layers to achieve proper compaction. IPC 306 covers bedding and backfill. Lifts that are too thick won't achieve required compaction density, leading to settlement and potential pipe damage.

Decision: fail (non-compliant work)
Risk: medium-high (settlement, pipe damage)
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant

Lift thickness - typical spec is 6-8 inch lifts for hand compaction, 12 inch for mechanical. If lifts are too thick, compaction won't reach bottom of lift.

events.label: response
events.text: {"decision":"fail","risk":"high","work_stage":"rough-complete","component_conditions":["installed-defective","non-compliant"],"percent_complete":75,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.85,"event_time":0.0,"rate":0.0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["Backfill lifts placed too thick for the compactor to achieve required density throughout the lift","Inadequate compaction will lead to settlement and potential pipe deformation or failure"],"actions":["Remove oversized lifts and re-place backfill in maximum 6-inch lifts for hand tampers or per spec for mechanical compaction","Compact each lift to required density before placing next lift","Verify bedding material gradation and depth meet IPC 306 before re-backfilling"],"rationale":"Lifts that exceed the compactor's effective depth cannot achieve uniform compaction at the bottom of the lift, violating IPC 306 bedding and backfill requirements and risking pipe damage from settlement or point loading.","references":["IPC 306","project specs"]}
